title: In Search of an Understandable Consensus Algorithm (Extended Version)
author: Diego Ongaro and John Ousterhout
note: The original Raft paper: leader election, log replication, safety, and membership changes, designed explicitly to be easier to understand than Paxos.

title: Raft: Understandable Distributed Consensus (The Secret Lives of Data)
author: Ben Johnson
note: Step-by-step animated visualization of Raft leader election and log replication, including what happens during network partitions.

title: Please stop calling databases CP or AP
author: Martin Kleppmann
note: Kleppmann's critique of the CAP theorem: its narrow definitions of consistency and availability mean most real databases are neither CP nor AP.

title: Consistency Models (Jepsen)
author: Kyle Kingsbury (Jepsen)
note: Reference map of consistency models showing how linearizability, sequential consistency, and serializability relate in a single hierarchy.

title: Time, Clocks, and the Ordering of Events in a Distributed System
author: Leslie Lamport
note: The 1978 paper introducing the happens-before relation and Lamport logical clocks, the foundation for the chapter's ID generator and ordering discussion.

title: MIT 6.5840 Lab 3: Raft
author: MIT PDOS (6.5840 Distributed Systems)
note: Hands-on lab implementing Raft in Go: leader election, log replication, persistence, and snapshotting, with rigorous failure-injection tests.

title: Distributed Systems lecture series (University of Cambridge)
author: Martin Kleppmann
note: Free Cambridge undergraduate lectures by the DDIA author covering logical time, broadcast ordering, replication, consensus, and Raft.

title: ZooKeeper: A Distributed Coordination Service for Distributed Applications
author: Apache Software Foundation
note: Official overview of ZooKeeper's znode data model, ordering guarantees, and watches, illustrating the coordination services section of the chapter.
